zh.lifeSummary: 當George Heard於1560年出生在英國英格蘭倫敦聖 波托爾福也斯 邱奇 阿爾蓋特時，他的父親George Heard20歲，母親Elizabeth Sewell19歲。他於大約1577年與Edna Perrin結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於大約1628年在英國英格蘭享年69歲。

nameMeanings.name: Heard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English herd(e), hird(e), hord(e), Older Scots herd, hird ‘herdsman’, i.e. one who looked after cattle, sheep, or goats (Old English heorde, hierde). Compare Hardman .

variants.name: Hirt
meanings: German: occupational name for a herdsman, Middle High German hirt, cognate with English Heard .

variants.name: Hardman
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English herdeman, herdema+n, hurdeman (Old English hierdeman, heordman) ‘herdsman, one who tends livestock’. Compare Heard .
English: occasionally from the Middle English personal name Hardman (Old English Heardmann, literally ‘hard man’).
English and Irish: shortened form of   Hardeman  .

variants.name: Heird
meanings: Scottish: probably a variant of   Heard  .

variants.name: Hard
meanings: English: variant of   Heard  . Compare   Hardman  .

English, German, Dutch, and Swedish (Hård): nickname for a stern or severe man, from Middle English, Middle Low German hard, Middle Dutch hart, hert, Swedish hård ‘hard, inflexible’. The Swedish name was probably originally a soldier's name.

English: topographic name denoting a dweller on hard, firm ground, from a transferred use of the adjective in 2 above, or perhaps sometimes from a landing-place, as in the modern Hampshire use of the term hard. Compare Hardacre .

variants.name: Herd
meanings: Scottish (Fife) and English: variant of   Heard  .

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

en.lifeSummary: When George Heard was born in 1560, in Saint Botolph's Church Aldgate, London, England, United Kingdom, his father, George Heard, was 20 and his mother, Elizabeth Sewell, was 19. He married Edna Perrin about 1577. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died about 1628, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 69.

ko.lifeSummary: George Heard(이)가 1560년 영국 잉글랜드 런던 세인트 벗프'스 c후르치 알드가트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 George Heard(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Sewell(은)는 19세였다. 그는 대략 1577년 Edna Perrin(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1628년 영국 잉글랜드에서 69세의 나이로 사망했다.
